channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Amazon will pay $2.25 million to settle FTC identity theft case
item.description: The retailer allegedly failed to meet requirements under the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

item.title: How to watch Mexico vs Ecuador: Free Streams, TV Channels & Kick-Off time as FIFA World Cup 2026 co-hosts try to end 40 years of hurt
item.description: Here's how to watch Mexico vs Ecuador for free online and from anywhere at the FIFA World Cup 2026, as co-hosts begin their knockout campaign.

item.title: 48 hours with the MemoMind One XR glasses — a slow AI, lack of a camera, and disappointing audio left me desperate for more
item.description: In a sea of smart glasses I can’t see why you’d choose one with no camera, slow AI, and several other faults over anything else.

item.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
item.description: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

item.title: I tested it, and Astell & Kern’s new pocketable smartphone DAC is a clear winner for detail, marred just slightly by bantamweight bass
item.description: Astell & Kern realises we can’t all afford one of its lovely digital audio players. So it’s trying to bring a little of the A&K magic to your humble smartphone…

item.title: Anthropic's new Sonnet 5 model is better at the tasks that are running up enterprise bills
item.description: Anthropic trained its newest Sonnet model to excel at agentic tasks, which have been causing a headache for the company's enterprise customers and power users.

item.title: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission fined Amazon $2.25 million to settle claims that the company failed to help customers who fell victim to identity theft, as reported earlier by Bloomberg. In its complaint, the FTC accuses Amazon of refusing to provide customers with information about purchases made with fraudulent accounts, in violation of the Fair Credit […]...

item.title: Daisy Sound’s First Headphones Are Premium, High-Quality—and Just a Little Bit Cheaper
item.description: The California-based startup enters a crowded headphone market with a $399 chiseled-aluminum gamble.

item.title: US Secret Service personnel are putting the lives of America’s VIPs at risk by refusing to use government-issued phones — but they might not be up to the job in the first place
item.description: The Secret Service isn't abiding by its own guidance, and it could be putting lives at risk.

item.title: Xbox layoff plans reportedly include closing Arkane, canceling Blade
item.description: The cuts will apparently start next week.

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: Netflix used AI to put Gene Wilder's voice into a new reality show
